title: PM UI Host-Browser-Validation Draft Packet History Demotion Execution
objective: Normalize the remaining live-looking 2026-04-19 PM UI host-browser-validation packet-definition JSON trio so those files no longer present themselves as still-live execution packets after standalone repo cutover and instead act as historical packet provenance with explicit current-routing redirects.
dependency_note: After Packet 194 closed the 2026-04-18 PM UI/read-surface packet-definition lane, the remaining adjacent residue in this branch is isolated to the 2026-04-19 host-browser-validation packet-definition trio from `pm-schema-ui-002e-host` through `pm-schema-ui-002g-host`. The corresponding parent-root handoff lane has already been demoted, so these packet JSONs are now the strongest current-looking residue inside that branch.
execution_scope: Retitle the PM UI host-browser-validation packet-definition trio as historical provenance.
Add explicit `historical_note` and `current_routing` fields to each packet JSON.
Preserve the original packet bodies below those top-entry routing fields without reopening runtime or implementation scope.
forbidden_scope: broad packet-archive rewriting beyond top-entry demotion and routing normalization
success_criteria: The PM UI host-browser-validation packet-definition trio now reads as historical provenance instead of live execution packets.
Each file explicitly routes current readers to `PROJECT_STATUS.md` and the closeout inventory.
Repo-owned status routing records the closure and leaves no ambiguity about the next remaining adjacent residue check.
next_action: Reassess any smaller adjacent packet-history surfaces that still read as current after the 2026-04-19 PM host-browser-validation trio closure.
